# CSS `:has()` Selector (Game Changer 🚀)

The `:has()` selector is often called the **CSS Parent Selector** because it allows you to style a parent element based on its children.

Before `:has()`, this usually required JavaScript.

***

# Basic Syntax

```css
parent:has(child) {
  /* styles */
}
```

***

# 1. Style Parent When Child Exists

### HTML

&lt;div class="card"&gt;&nbsp; &lt;button&gt;Save&lt;/button&gt;&lt;/div&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

### CSS

```css
.card:has(button) {
  border: 2px solid green;
}
```

Output:

```text
Card gets green border because it contains a button.
```

***

# 2. Form Validation (Very Common Interview Question)

### HTML

&lt;div class="form-group"&gt;&nbsp; &lt;input&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; type="email"&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; required&nbsp; /&gt;&lt;/div&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

### CSS

```css
.form-group:has(input:invalid) {
  border: 2px solid red;
}

.form-group:has(input:valid) {
  border: 2px solid green;
}
```

No JavaScript needed ✅

***

# React Example

```jsx
function Form() {
  return (
    <div className="form-group">
      <input
        type="email"
        required
      />
    </div>
  );
}
```

```css
.form-group:has(input:invalid) {
  background: #fee2e2;
}

.form-group:has(input:valid) {
  background: #dcfce7;
}
```

***

# 3. Highlight Card When Button is Hovered

### React

```jsx
<div className="card">
  <h3>React Course</h3>

  <button>
    Enrol
  </button>
</div>
```

### CSS

```css
.card:has(button:hover) {
  transform: scale(1.05);
  box-shadow:
    0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,.2);
}
```

When user hovers button:

```text
Whole card gets highlighted
```

***

# 4. Checkbox Toggle Without JavaScript

### HTML

&lt;div class="settings"&gt;&nbsp; &lt;input&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; type="checkbox"&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; id="dark"&nbsp; /&gt;&nbsp; &lt;label for="dark"&gt;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Dark Mode&nbsp; &lt;/label&gt;&lt;/div&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

### CSS

```css
.settings:has(
  input:checked
) {
  background: black;
  color: white;
}
```

***

# React Example

```jsx
<div className="settings">
  <input
    type="checkbox"
    id="dark"
  />

  <label htmlFor="dark">
    Dark Mode
  </label>
</div>
```

***

# 5. Active Navigation Item

### React

```jsx
<nav>
  <a href="/">Home</a>
  <a
    href="/">
    About
  </a>
</nav>
```

### CSS

```css
nav:has(.active) {
  border-bottom:
    3px solid blue;
}
```

***

# 6. Table Row Highlight

### HTML

&lt;tr&gt;&nbsp; &lt;td&gt;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; &lt;input&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; type="checkbox"&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; /&gt;&nbsp; &lt;/td&gt;&nbsp; &lt;td&gt;Sudhir&lt;/td&gt;&lt;/tr&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

### CSS

```css
tr:has(
  input:checked
) {
  background-color:
    lightblue;
}
```

Perfect for:

```text
Data Tables
Grids
Admin Panels
```

***

# 7. Shopping Cart Example

### React

```jsx
<div className="product">
  <input
    type="checkbox"
  />

  <h3>MacBook Pro</h3>
</div>
```

### CSS

```css
.product:has(
  input:checked
) {
  border:
    3px solid green;
}
```

***

# 8. Empty State Detection

### HTML

&lt;div class="results"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

### CSS

```css
.results:not(:has(*)) {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: center;
}
```

Meaning:

```text
If results has no child elements
```

apply styles.

***

# 9. Multiple Conditions

```css
.card:has(
  img
):has(
  button:hover
) {
  border:
    2px solid blue;
}
```

Meaning:

```text
Card contains image
AND
button is hovered
```

***

# 10. Modern React Dashboard Example

### JSX

```jsx
<div className="widget">
  <input
    type="checkbox"
  />

  <div className="content">
    Dashboard Widget
  </div>
</div>
```

### CSS

```css
.widget {
  padding: 1rem;
  border: 1px solid #ddd;
}

.widget:has(
  input:checked
) {
  border-color: green;
  background: #f0fdf4;
}
```

***

# Before vs After `:has()`

### Before

```javascript
const checkbox =
  document.querySelector(
    "input"
  );

checkbox.addEventListener(
  "change",
  () => {
    checkbox.parentElement
      .classList
      .toggle("active");
  }
);
```

Required JavaScript ❌

***

### Now

```css
.parent:has(
  input:checked
)
```

Pure CSS ✅

***

# Interview Examples

### Style Form on Error

```css
.form-group:has(
  input:invalid
)
```

***

### Style Parent When Child Focused

```css
.card:has(
  input:focus
)
```

***

### Style Table Row Selection

```css
tr:has(
  input:checked
)
```

***

### Style Accordion

```css
.accordion:has(
  button[aria-expanded="true"]
)
```

***

# Senior Frontend Interview Answer

> The `:has()` selector is a relational pseudo-class that allows CSS to select an element based on its descendants, children, or sibling conditions. It effectively acts as a parent selector and enables patterns such as form validation styling, checkbox-driven UI states, active navigation highlighting, and interactive card components without requiring JavaScript. It is one of the most powerful modern CSS features because it reduces DOM manipulation and simplifies component styling in React applications.
